newsletter.body: One bulletin a month, no ads, unsubscribe anytime. Exclusively for QS customers and partners.

article.intro: Ho Chi Minh City, 28/04/2026 — QS Technology today officially announced the Astro 12X, the newest controller in the Astro-series family, capable of controlling 12 servo axes simultaneously over real-time EtherCAT. It is QS's flagship product, aimed at complex automation lines such as multi-axis machining centres, industrial robots, and automatic feeding systems.
article.intro2: Compared with the current flagship Astro 10i with 6 synchronised axes, the Astro 12X doubles the axis count, cuts the EtherCAT cycle from 500 µs to 250 µs, and integrates a machine-vision module via GigE Vision — enabling pick-and-place and inline quality-inspection applications without third-party equipment.

paras: In QS's 2025 OEM customer survey, 43% of the 120 integrators polled said they were having to combine 2 controllers to get enough axes for their line — especially 5-axis CNC machines with an automatic tool changer (ATC), or feeding lines with multiple robot arms.

quote.text: We wanted to eliminate the situation where a customer has to use 2 separate controllers synced over a slow bus. The Astro 12X is the answer — a single controller for the entire line.
quote.cite: — Lê Thanh Mai, CTO QS Technology

after: The Astro 12X was developed over 18 months, with more than 400 hours of real-world testing at 3 partner factories in Ho Chi Minh City, Long An, and Hai Phong. The unit on display at VME 2026 is a pilot that has run continuously for 720 hours on an 8-axis turn-mill line without a single fault.

list: 12 servo axes synchronised over EtherCAT, selectable 250 µs / 500 µs cycle
12.1" touchscreen at 1280×800, Gorilla Glass, IP65 oil-resistant front
Integrated GigE Vision machine-vision module, supporting Basler / Hikvision cameras up to 5 MP
64 GB industrial SSD program memory, storing up to 12,000 programs
2× Gigabit Ethernet, Wi-Fi 6, Modbus TCP, OPC UA, MQTT connectivity
CNC-machined aluminium housing with passive cooling, fanless — operates 0–55°C

paras: Ordering opens from 15/05/2026, with priority for customers already running the Astro 10i and F86. The first batch of 60 units is expected to ship in September 2026, with steady capacity of 80 units/month from Q4. The standard version is listed at 89 million VND, including a 24-month warranty and 3 days of operator training at the QS factory.

The Astro 12X will be exhibited and demonstrated live at VME 2026, Booth A23, SECC HCMC, from 22–25/04/2026. Customers who want an in-depth configuration discussion can book a private session with the technical team via support@qstcnc.com or hotline (+84) 909.663.350.
